what_it_does: A static preview card (@dsCard group="Brand") documenting voice & tone: it states the voice (precise, second-person, sentence-case) and shows a 2x2 grid of Use/Avoid example copy pairs styled with success/error tag colors.
observations: Pure static HTML, no scripts — only _card.css linked.
Two 'Use' examples (sentence-case, direct 'you', inline URL preview) and two 'Avoid' examples (exclamation+emoji, all-caps).
Lead text states the rules: inline previews of consequences, direct 'you', no marketing voice, no exclamation marks, no emoji in copy.
URL preview example uses --accent-gold for the accent span.
standouts: The 'Avoid' examples deliberately contain an emoji and all-caps to demonstrate the anti-patterns the voice forbids.
Inline URL preview (conceptv.io/panotours/...) is used as a positive example of showing consequences inline.
conventions: Uses status tokens (--status-success / --status-error) for do/avoid semantics.
notes: The brand voice card — a do/avoid copy guide codifying ConceptV's precise, second-person, sentence-case tone.
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — subtitle (precise, second-person, sentence-case examples) is exactly what the card presents.
apparent_themes: Voice as a single-sourced brand rule
intention_signals: Codifies the product's writing voice as part of the design system so all copy stays precise, second-person, and free of marketing/emoji noise — voice treated as a design token in prose form.
novelty: Including written voice & tone as a first-class brand card alongside visual tokens, with concrete app-specific use/avoid examples.
contribution: Contributes the canonical voice & tone specification (precise, second-person, sentence-case, inline consequences) with do/avoid examples that a fused system would keep as its copy-writing rule.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV brand preview card specifying voice & tone — precise, second-person, sentence-case — with a 2x2 grid of Use/Avoid copy examples styled by success/error status tokens.
intention_for_embedding: To codify the product's writing voice as a single-sourced brand rule guiding all UI copy.
